page: UpCloud HIMEM-4xCPU-64GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 shared vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a 200 GB SSD, and the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate a clear tradeoff between memory capacity and raw computational throughput. The shared AMD EPYC processor delivers poor single-core performance with a stress-ng score of 787.09 and weak multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 3129.46. While cached small-block memory read bandwidth is average at 408.34 GB/sec, uncached large-block read and write bandwidths are poor, measuring 13.55 GB/sec and 9.91 GB/sec respectively. Passmark CPU tests show average performance for extended instructions and encryption, but database operations and Redis SET throughput remain weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server provides high memory density relative to its CPU allocation, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive, sustained CPU performance. It is suited for caching layers, lightweight database hosting, and memory-intensive text processing. However, it is less suitable for CPU-heavy tasks like software compilation, ray tracing, or large-scale LLM inference, where its shared CPU architecture and weak multi-core scores present performance bottlenecks.
description: UpCloud HIMEM-4xCPU-64GB is a KVM-based virtual server designed for memory-intensive workloads. It features 4 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM and a local 200 GB SSD. The server offers a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. Benchmark data reveals a performance profile characterized by average cached memory read bandwidth of 408.34 GB/sec and average single-core Geekbench scores, contrasted with weak multi-core CPU performance and poor uncached memory bandwidth. This hardware configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy applications that require a large memory footprint without demanding high CPU compute density. It is best suited for caching systems, lightweight databases, and memory-bound text processing tasks, while being less optimal for CPU-intensive operations like software compilation or complex LLM inference.
og_description: UpCloud HIMEM-4xCPU-64GB is a KVM virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 200 GB SSD, offering high memory density for caching and lightweight database workloads.
